title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal risk; however, improper implementation or reliance on it without proper validation could lead to missed vulnerabilities or false assurances in dependency management.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment to ensure its accuracy and reliability before integrating it into production workflows.
Use the tool as a supplementary measure alongside other established vulnerability scanning solutions to ensure comprehensive coverage.
Regularly monitor and update the scanner to ensure it remains effective against newly discovered vulnerabilities.

title: The Pre* Party Resource Hints plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the ...
description: The Pre* Party Resource Hints plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the 'hint_ids' parameter of the pprh_update_hints AJAX action in all versions up to, and including, 1.8.20. This is due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries that can be used to e

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with at least Subscriber-level access to exploit SQL Injection, potentially leading to unauthorized data access, data modification, or database compromise. This could result in data breaches, data loss, or service disruption.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or disable the Pre* Party Resource Hints plugin until a patched version is released.
Restrict access to the WordPress admin panel to trusted users and review user roles and permissions to limit Subscriber-level access.
Monitor database activity for unusual behavior and implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious SQL queries.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in PbootCMS up to 3.2.12. This affects an unknown function of...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in PbootCMS up to 3.2.12. This affects an unknown function of the file core/function/file.php of the component File Upload. The manipulation of the argument black results in incomplete blacklist. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4509.

title: The Speedup Optimization plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in all...
description: The Speedup Optimization plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in all versions up to and including 1.5.9. The `speedup01_ajax_enabled()` function, which handles the `wp_ajax_speedup01_enabled` AJAX action, does not perform any capability check via `current_user_can()` and also lacks nonce verification. This is in contrast to other AJAX handlers in the same plugin (e.g., `speedup01_ajax_install_iox` and `speedup01_ajax_delete_cache_file`) which properly check for `install_pl

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4127.

title: The Show Posts list – Easy designs, filters and more plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored...
description: The Show Posts list – Easy designs, filters and more plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'post_type' shortcode attribute in the 'swiftpost-list' shortcode in all versions up to, and including, 1.1.0 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user-supplied attributes. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesse

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with contributor-level access or higher to inject malicious scripts into pages via the 'post_type' shortcode attribute. This could lead to stored cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks, potentially compromising user data, session cookies, or enabling further attacks on site visitors.
recommended_actions: Update the 'Show Posts list – Easy designs, filters and more' plugin to a patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict user roles and permissions to limit contributor-level access to trusted individuals only.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to help detect and block malicious script injection attempts.

title: The Ecover Builder For Dummies plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting...
description: The Ecover Builder For Dummies plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'id' parameter of the 'ecover' shortcode in all versions up to and including 1.0. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on the user-supplied 'id' shortcode attribute. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4077.

title: The Appmax plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Improper Input Validation in all versions up to,...
description: The Appmax plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Improper Input Validation in all versions up to, and including, 1.0.3. This is due to the plugin registering a public REST API webhook endpoint at /webhook-system without implementing webhook signature validation, secret verification, or any mechanism to authenticate that incoming webhook requests genuinely originate from the legitimate Appmax payment service. The plugin directly processes untrusted attacker-controlled input from the 'event' and '

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Appmax plugin for WordPress allows attackers to exploit the improperly validated webhook endpoint, potentially enabling unauthorized actions such as data manipulation, fraudulent transactions, or service disruptions. This could lead to financial loss, reputational damage, or unauthorized access to sensitive data.
affected_industries: Nonprofits using WordPress for payment processing
recommended_actions: Immediately update or disable the Appmax plugin until a patched version is released that addresses the improper input validation vulnerability.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to monitor and block malicious requests targeting the vulnerable webhook endpoint.
Audit recent transactions and logs for any suspicious activity that may indicate exploitation of this vulnerability.

title: The Review Map by RevuKangaroo plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting...
description: The Review Map by RevuKangaroo plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the plugin settings in all versions up to, and including, 1.7 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with administrator-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page. This only affects multi-site installations and installations where unfiltered_ht

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4161.

title: The Alfie – Feed Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the...
description: The Alfie – Feed Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'naam' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 1.2.1. This is due to missing nonce validation on the alfie_option_page() function combined with insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject malicious web scripts that will be stored in the plugin's database and execute whenever a user accesses the page displaying the inject

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4069.

title: The Task Manager plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary shortcode execution via the ...
description: The Task Manager plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary shortcode execution via the 'search' AJAX action in all versions up to, and including, 3.0.2. This is due to missing capability checks in the callback_search() function and insufficient input validation that allows shortcode syntax (square brackets) to pass through sanitize_text_field() and be concatenated into a do_shortcode() call. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to exec

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4004.

title: The WP Random Button plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the ...
description: The WP Random Button plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'cat', 'nocat', and 'text' shortcode attributes of the 'wp_random_button' shortcode in all versions up to, and including, 1.0. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user-supplied shortcode attributes. Specifically, the random_button_html() function directly concatenates the 'cat' and 'nocat' parameters into HTML data-attributes without esc_attr(), and the 'text' paramete

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to inject malicious JavaScript code into the website, which can execute in the context of other users' browsers. This could lead to session hijacking, credential theft, or the delivery of further malicious payloads. The impact is particularly concerning for websites with high user interaction or sensitive user data.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or disable the WP Random Button plugin until a patched version is released.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious payloads targeting this vulnerability.
Perform a security audit of your WordPress site to identify and mitigate other potential vulnerabilities.

title: The Neos Connector for Fakturama plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery...
description: The Neos Connector for Fakturama plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery in all versions up to and including 0.0.14. This is due to missing nonce validation in the ncff_add_plugin_page() function which handles settings updates. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to modify plugin settings via a forged request, granted they can trick a site administrator into performing an action such as clicking a link.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4143.

title: The Build App Online plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access in all versions up...
description: The Build App Online plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access in all versions up to, and including, 1.0.23. This is due to the plugin registering the 'build-app-online-update-vendor-product' AJAX action via wp_ajax_nopriv_ without proper authentication checks, capability verification, or nonce validation in the update_vendor_product() function. The function accepts a user-supplied post ID from the request and calls wp_update_post() to modify the post_author field without validat

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized users to modify the post_author field of any post, potentially leading to privilege escalation, unauthorized content manipulation, or impersonation of legitimate users. This could compromise the integrity and trustworthiness of the affected WordPress site.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or disable the Build App Online plugin if it is in use, and monitor the plugin's repository for a patched version.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to block unauthorized access to vulnerable AJAX actions.
Review and monitor user accounts and post changes for any unauthorized modifications, and revert any suspicious changes.

title: The fyyd podcast shortcodes plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via...
description: The fyyd podcast shortcodes plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'fyyd-podcast', 'fyyd-episode', and 'fyyd' shortcodes in all versions up to, and including, 0.3.1. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user-supplied shortcode attributes such as 'color', 'podcast_id', and 'podcast_slug'. These attributes are directly concatenated into inline JavaScript within single-quoted string arguments without any escaping or sanitization, a

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4084.

title: The WP Games Embed plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the ...
description: The WP Games Embed plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the [game] shortcode in all versions up to and including 0.1beta. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user-supplied shortcode attributes such as 'width', 'height', 'src', 'title', 'description', 'game_url', 'main', and 'thumb', which are all directly concatenated into HTML output without any escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with Contributor-level access to inject malicious scripts into the website via the WP Games Embed plugin. These scripts could execute in the context of other users, including administrators, leading to potential data theft, session hijacking, or further compromise of the site.
recommended_actions: Update or disable the WP Games Embed plugin until a patched version is released.
Restrict Contributor-level access to trusted users only and review user roles and permissions.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious payloads targeting this vulnerability.

title: The Ad Short plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'ad'...
description: The Ad Short plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'ad' shortcode's 'client' attribute in all versions up to and including 2.0.1. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on the 'client' shortcode attribute. The ad_func() shortcode handler at line 71 accepts a 'client' attribute via shortcode_atts() and directly concatenates it into a double-quoted HTML attribute (data-ad-client) at line 130 without applying esc_attr() or any other sa

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4067.

title: The Sheets2Table plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the ...
description: The Sheets2Table plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'titles' shortcode attribute in the [sheets2table-render-table] shortcode in all versions up to and including 0.4.1. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. Specifically, the 'titles' attribute value from the shortcode is passed through S2T_Functions::trim_array_values() (which only trims whitespace) and then echoed directly into HTML via `echo $header` inside a <th> tag in the

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into the 'titles' attribute of the [sheets2table-render-table] shortcode. This could lead to Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks, enabling attackers to steal sensitive user data, hijack user sessions, or perform unauthorized actions on behalf of users who view the compromised page.
recommended_actions: Update the Sheets2Table plugin to a patched version as soon as an update is released by the developer.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious input targeting this vulnerability.
Review and sanitize user inputs thoroughly, especially for attributes like 'titles' in shortcodes, to prevent injection attacks.

title: The Multi Functional Flexi Lightbox plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site...
description: The Multi Functional Flexi Lightbox plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the `arv_lb[message]` parameter in all versions up to, and including, 1.2 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This is due to the `arv_lb_options_val()` sanitize callback returning user input without any sanitization, and the stored `message` value being output in the `genLB()` function without escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Administ

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3347.

title: The Expire Users plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in all versions up to...
description: The Expire Users plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in all versions up to, and including, 1.2.2. This is due to the plugin allowing a user to update the 'on_expire_default_to_role' meta through the 'save_extra_user_profile_fields' function. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to elevate their privileges to that of an administrator.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4261.

title: The REST API TO MiniProgram plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object...
description: The REST API TO MiniProgram plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in all versions up to, and including, 5.1.2. This is due to the permission callback (update_user_wechatshop_info_permissions_check) only validating that the supplied 'openid' parameter corresponds to an existing WordPress user, while the callback function (update_user_wechatshop_info) uses a separate, attacker-controlled 'userid' parameter to determine which user's metadata gets modified, with no v

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3460.

title: The WP-Chatbot for Messenger plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all...
description: The WP-Chatbot for Messenger plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 4.9. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to overwrite the site's MobileMonkey API token and company ID options, which can be used to hijack chatbot configuration and redirect visitor conversations to an attacker-controlled MobileMonkey account.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized attackers to hijack chatbot configurations, potentially redirecting sensitive visitor conversations to attacker-controlled accounts. This could lead to data leakage, reputational damage, and loss of customer trust.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the WP-Chatbot for Messenger plugin to the latest patched version if available.
Audit and monitor chatbot configurations for unauthorized changes or suspicious activity.
Implement additional access controls and authentication mechanisms for administrative actions on the WordPress site.

title: CVE-2016-20027 - ZKTeco ZKBioSecurity 3.0 contains multiple reflected cross-site scripting vulnerabilities that allow
description: ZKTeco ZKBioSecurity 3.0 contains multiple reflected cross-site scripting vulnerabilities that allow attackers to execute arbitrary HTML and script code by injecting malicious payloads through unsanitized parameters in multiple scripts. Attackers can craft malicious URLs with XSS payloads in vulnerable parameters to execute scripts in a user's browser session within the context of the affected application.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The reflected cross-site scripting vulnerabilities in ZKTeco ZKBioSecurity 3.0 could allow attackers to execute arbitrary scripts in users' browsers, potentially leading to session hijacking, data theft, or phishing attacks. This poses a risk to the confidentiality and integrity of user data.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest patches or updates provided by ZKTeco to address the vulnerabilities.
Implement input validation and sanitization for all user-supplied parameters to prevent injection attacks.
Educate users to avoid clicking on suspicious links and implement Content Security Policy (CSP) to mitigate XSS attacks.

title: CVE-2016-20026 - ZKTeco ZKBioSecurity 3.0 contains hardcoded credentials in the bundled Apache Tomcat server that all
description: ZKTeco ZKBioSecurity 3.0 contains hardcoded credentials in the bundled Apache Tomcat server that allow unauthenticated attackers to access the manager application. Attackers can authenticate with hardcoded credentials stored in tomcat-users.xml to upload malicious WAR archives containing JSP applications and execute arbitrary code with SYSTEM privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2016-20026.

title: CVE-2016-20025 - ZKTeco ZKAccess Professional 3.5.3 contains an insecure file permissions vulnerability that allows a
description: ZKTeco ZKAccess Professional 3.5.3 contains an insecure file permissions vulnerability that allows authenticated users to escalate privileges by modifying executable files. Attackers can leverage the Modify permission granted to the Authenticated Users group to replace executable binaries with malicious code for privilege escalation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2016-20025.

title: CVE-2016-20024 - ZKTeco ZKTime.Net 3.0.1.6 contains an insecure file permissions vulnerability that allows unprivileg
description: ZKTeco ZKTime.Net 3.0.1.6 contains an insecure file permissions vulnerability that allows unprivileged users to escalate privileges by modifying executable files. Attackers can exploit world-writable permissions on the ZKTimeNet3.0 directory and its contents to replace executable files with malicious binaries for privilege escalation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2016-20024.

title: CVE-2015-20121 - Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains SQL injection vulnerabilities that allow unauthentic
description: Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains SQL injection vulnerabilities that allow unauthenticated attackers to manipulate database queries by injecting arbitrary SQL code through the GET parameter 'u_id' in /admin/users.php and the POST parameter 'agent[]' in /admin/mailer.php. Attackers can exploit time-based blind SQL injection techniques to extract sensitive database information or cause denial of service through sleep-based payloads.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2015-20121.

title: CVE-2015-20120 - Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains multiple time-based blind SQL injection vulnerabilit
description: Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains multiple time-based blind SQL injection vulnerabilities that allow unauthenticated attackers to extract database information by injecting SQL code into application parameters. Attackers can craft requests with time-delay payloads to infer database contents character by character based on response timing differences.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2015-20120.

title: CVE-2015-20119 - Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that all
description: Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to inject malicious HTML and iframe elements through the text parameter in the pages.php admin interface. Attackers can submit POST requests to the add page action with crafted iframe payloads in the text parameter to store malicious content that executes in the browsers of users viewing the affected pages.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2015-20119.

title: CVE-2015-20118 - Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in the l
description: Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in the location_name parameter of the admin locations interface. Attackers can submit POST requests to the locations.php endpoint with JavaScript payloads in the location_name field to execute arbitrary code in administrator browsers.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2015-20118.

title: CVE-2015-20117 - Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerability that allo
description: Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to create unauthorized user accounts and administrative users by crafting malicious forms. Attackers can submit hidden form data to /admin/addusers.php and /admin/editadmins.php endpoints to register new users with arbitrary credentials and escalate privileges to SUPERUSER level.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2015-20117.

title: CVE-2015-20116 - Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 fails to properly sanitize CSV file uploads, allowing attacke
description: Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 fails to properly sanitize CSV file uploads, allowing attackers to inject malicious scripts through filename parameters in multipart form data. Attackers can upload files with XSS payloads in the filename field to execute arbitrary JavaScript in users' browsers when the file is processed or displayed.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2015-20116.

title: CVE-2015-20115 - Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 fails to properly sanitize file uploads, allowing attackers t
description: Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 fails to properly sanitize file uploads, allowing attackers to store malicious scripts through the file POST parameter in admin/tools.php. Attackers can upload files containing JavaScript code that executes in the context of admin/tools.php when accessed by other users.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2015-20115.

title: CVE-2015-20114 - Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows att
description: Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows attackers to execute arbitrary HTML and script code by injecting malicious input through multiple parameters that are not properly sanitized. Attackers can craft requests with injected script payloads in vulnerable parameters to execute code in users' browser sessions within the context of the affected application.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2015-20114.

title: CVE-2015-20113 - Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains cross-site request forgery and persistent cross-site
description: Next Click Ventures RealtyScript 4.0.2 contains cross-site request forgery and persistent cross-site scripting vulnerabilities that allow attackers to perform administrative actions and inject malicious scripts. Attackers can craft malicious web pages that execute unauthorized actions when logged-in users visit them, or inject persistent scripts that execute in the application context.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2015-20113.

title: CVE-2013-20006 - Qool CMS contains multiple persistent cross-site scripting vulnerabilities in several administrative
description: Qool CMS contains multiple persistent cross-site scripting vulnerabilities in several administrative scripts where POST parameters are not properly sanitized before being stored and returned to users. Attackers can inject malicious JavaScript code through parameters like 'title', 'name', 'email', 'username', 'link', and 'task' in endpoints such as addnewtype, addnewdatafield, addmenu, addusergroup, addnewuserfield, adduser, addgeneraldata, and addcontentitem to execute arbitrary scripts in administrator browsers.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2013-20006.

title: CVE-2013-20005 - Qool CMS 2.0 RC2 contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerability that allows attackers to perfor
description: Qool CMS 2.0 RC2 contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerability that allows attackers to perform administrative actions by tricking logged-in users into visiting malicious web pages. Attackers can forge POST requests to the /admin/adduser endpoint with parameters like username, password, email, and level to create root-level user accounts without user consent.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2013-20005.
